_comment: Phase G (G8b / C7) — the SINGLE source of truth for the survival-mesh transports. The three OS front-ends (bootstrap.sh, bootstrap.command, bootstrap.ps1) read THIS file so transport facts are never hardcoded in three places (the same drift lesson as languages.py generating both PHP and JS locale registries). Each transport key matches App\Models\FederationTransport::TRANSPORTS exactly. `self_advert` is a template the operator fills in; `env` is the .env key the transport needs (null = none). `needs_host_daemon` flags the channels whose install modifies the host OS — those are certified on the physical rig, never on amd64/Docker-Desktop alone.

https.label: Clearnet HTTPS
https.configure: Expose the nginx port publicly (reverse proxy / port-forward / tunnel) and advertise the reachable https URL.

onion.label: Tor hidden service (censorship-resistant)

install.linux: sudo apt-get install -y tor   # or: sudo dnf install -y tor

onion.configure: Add a HiddenServiceDir + HiddenServicePort 80 127.0.0.1:<nginx-port> to torrc, restart tor, read hostname; the SOCKS proxy is the env value below.

yggdrasil.label: Yggdrasil overlay (self-routing IPv6, no DNS / no static IP)
yggdrasil.configure: Generate a config, add public peers (https://github.com/yggdrasil-network/public-peers), start the daemon, read the [200::]/7 address; routes directly (no SOCKS).

sneakernet.label: Offline bundle (USB / air-gap export-import)
sneakernet.configure: No live channel — partition export/import bundles are carried by hand. Advertise nothing; this transport is recorded for provenance only.
